title: Jet Ski Rental in Dallas: Prices, Seasons, and Tips for 2026
html:
Jet ski rental in Dallas offers an exhilarating way to explore Lake Lewisville, with prices from around EUR 200 for a two-hour session. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators across the region, making it simple to book a watercraft for your chosen date.

How much does it cost to rent a jet ski in Dallas?

A two-hour jet ski rental in Dallas typically ranges from EUR 180 to EUR 250, with full-day options costing EUR 350 to EUR 500. Prices vary by season and model. Shared guided tours start around EUR 100 per person for a one-hour ride. You can check live rates and find a skippered charter for larger groups on BoatHire24.

Where to set off in Dallas

Most jet ski rentals depart from marinas on Lake Lewisville, the primary waterbody for powerboating near Dallas. Key launch points include Hidden Cove Park Marina, Lake Dallas Marina, and Westlake Park. For a longer ride, head towards the islands at Little Elm Park or explore the coves near Hickory Creek. Lewisville Lake Environmental Learning Area offers a quieter launch. All locations are within a 40-minute drive of downtown Dallas.

Which boat to rent

  1. Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski, Best for comfort and speed, seating up to three passengers, from EUR 200 for two hours. You can book the Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski for hire directly from owners on BoatHire24.
  2. Yamaha VX Cruiser, Great for families, with a relaxed ride and storage for gear, seats three, from EUR 180 for two hours.
  3. Kawasaki Ultra LX, A touring model with extended range, ideal for half-day trips, seats three, from EUR 350 for a four-hour hire.
  4. Spark TRIXX, Lightweight and nimble, suitable for solo riders or pairs, from EUR 150 for two hours.

How much does a full-day rental cost?

  • Shared guided tour (one hour): from EUR 80 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (four hours): from EUR 300.
  • Catamaran full-day (eight hours): from EUR 800.
  • Crewed yacht full-day: from EUR 1,500 for up to six guests.

When is the best season and do you need a licence?

The peak season for jet ski rental in Dallas runs from May to September, with July and August being the busiest months due to warm weather. Winds from the south can create choppy conditions on Lake Lewisville, so check forecasts before heading out. Regarding licences, Texas law requires anyone born after 1 September 1993 to hold a boater education card to operate a personal watercraft. The rental operator will confirm exact requirements for your trip.

On windy days, head to the sheltered coves near Hidden Cove Park for a smoother ride; the main channel can get busy with wake boats.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 80, 500 per session.
  • Peak months: May, September.
  • Trip length: 1, 8 hours.
  • Capacity: up to three passengers per jet ski.
  • Currency: EUR.
  • Distance: Lake Lewisville is 30 nautical miles north of downtown Dallas.

For information on how bookings and payments work, refer to the booking FAQ on BoatHire24.

Questions about jet ski rental in Dallas

Can I rent a jet ski without a licence in Dallas?

You can rent a jet ski without a licence if you are over 18 and have completed a boater education course if required.

Is a deposit required for jet ski rental?

Most operators require a security deposit of EUR 200 to EUR 500, refunded after the rental if no damage occurs.

What is the minimum age to drive a jet ski in Dallas?

The minimum age is 14 to drive, but renters must be at least 18 to sign the contract.

Can I take a jet ski from Lake Lewisville to other lakes?

No, jet skis are typically restricted to Lake Lewisville due to transport and insurance limitations.

In 2026, booking a jet ski in Dallas is straightforward on BoatHire24, where you can compare options and check availability for your dates. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Dallas and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
